title: e2e tests: enable golint
body:
...and fix problems found therewith

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This is going to fail. There are still some lint failures that I'll need help with. And it's going to need pretty heavy review.
Oh dear; I hadn't looked in `pkg/bindings/test`. This will all have to wait.

Link to [result](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5425554955436032). I fixed the trivial whitespace error; am curious why `golangci-lint` doesn't issue that warning on my laptop.

Am very curious about the dot and underscore errors. All the code in `tes/e2e` uses dot imports and `package foo_bar` all over the place. Where/how are those errors silenced?

The `unkeyed data fields`, I tried to fix, gave up.

You are brave, @edsantiago.  Thank you so much for doing this!

> Am very curious about the dot and underscore errors. All the code in `tes/e2e` uses dot imports and `package foo_bar` all over the place. Where/how are those errors silenced?

Does `// nolint` work?

`//nolint` works in some places; I use it for the ALL_CAPS warnings. 

My question is: `test/e2e` uses dot-imports and `package foo_bar` everywhere, *without* `//nolint`, and there are no lint errors. How? And how do I do the same in `pkg/bindings/test`?
@edsantiago I think you have to rename the package to `bindings_test`. The `_test` name is a special case to indicate that this is a test only package.

The `composite literal uses unkeyed fields` error says that you assigning values to the struct without specifying the key name, e.g.
`podmanSession := &PodmanSession{session}` should be `podmanSession := &PodmanSession{Session: session}`

Yeah... that's the first thing I tried. It made no difference. (Or it may have just changed to a different error message; I can't remember any more). I'll look into it again next week.

Thanks for the `foo_test` vs `test_foo` hint. I'll try that next week too.
It's huge, but most of the changes are fairly trivial, and all that I saw LGTM

Thanks. What do you recommend? Should I wait for the FIXMEs to be fixed, or file issues for them and reference them in the FIXME comments? (I filed #12436 for the runlabel one but not for the bindings tests)
We should work the bindings issue out (maybe at scrum?) but I'm fine merging with FIXMEs as long as we have issues created for them.

Done. Issues filed. Comments and skips updated. Rebased. Two flakes restarted, but I gotta go. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL. (Any failures now are 100% guaranteed to be flakes. All e2e tests have passed as of this writing).
